bullets.text: Full-year 2018 combined ratio of 90.6 with 20% NPW growth; Personal Lines CR 90.3, Commercial Lines CR 86.7; Property CR 106.9 due to $295 million in retained catastrophe losses equaling 23 combined ratio points.
Net premiums written crossed $30 billion, adding over $5 billion in 2018 after $3.8 billion in 2017 and $2.8 billion in 2016; Commercial Lines NPW grew nearly 30%; Property NPW grew 33%.

Auto PIFs grew 14% to 13 million; companywide PIFs surpassed 20 million; Robinson customers reached 1 million; the latest million auto PIFs added in just six months, the fastest pace in company history.

Expense ratio of 20.4% among the lowest in the industry; Personal Lines non-acquisition expense ratio improved 0.7 points to 9.1%; loss adjustment expense ratio declined 0.7 points to 10.2%; advertising topped $1.4 billion.

Raised $1.1 billion in March and $550 million in October via senior notes and preferred stock; 2018 investment portfolio returned 1.2% with fixed income earning 1.5% and equities returning negative 4.4%; portfolio grew to over $33 billion.

Property rates increased to address inadequacy; Gainshare score of 1.91, best since 2004; dividend policy revised from formulaic Gainshare-based variable to fixed quarterly $0.10 per share plus annual variable component.

Launched 'Ask Flo' virtual assistant in mobile app; deployed AI-powered Docuflash document classification; replaced legacy IVR with natural-language voice system; new web servicing stack completed with 4x mobile engagement growth since 2016.

Portfolio quoting live for all agents in three states with countrywide target by mid-2020; HomeQuote Explorer and BusinessQuote Explorer established as leading digital insurance marketplaces; media impressions for auto/home bundle doubled vs. 2015.

Progressive articulated a three-Horizons investment framework: Horizon 1 Execute core growth, Horizon 2 Expand into adjacencies (BOP, Uber expansion to 13 states), Horizon 3 Explore via new Strategy team under Chief Strategy Officer.

Hurricane Michael retained losses limited to $60 million via reinsurance; total 2018 catastrophe retained losses including hurricanes, hail, and California wildfires totaled $295 million, driving Property CR to 106.9.
